journal.body:
# Journal, Wednesday, 27 May 2026

## Recovery (Garmin, last night)

- Sleep score: **76 (FAIR)**, total logged **9h 0m** (deep 88m / REM 121m / light 331m / awake 41m) — **but the number is misleading.** Garmin only counts from when I finally settled. It can't see the hours I lay awake fighting it before ~3am. Real overnight rest was ~2h; the morning recovery sleep (to 09:30) is what rescued the score.
- HRV last night: **40** (weekly 45, **BALANCED**) — slightly down, not alarmed.
- Training readiness: **55 (MODERATE)** — flagged low-sleep-score + negative-stress-history. Matches the night exactly.
- Body battery: charged 48 / drained 30.

**Reflection on these numbers:** The body clawed back more than 2h of sleep should allow because I went back to bed instead of forcing the bike. That decision is the only reason I'm at 55 and functional rather than wrecked. The headline 76 / 9h flatters a genuinely bad night.

## IPSATIVE goals

- **Yesterday's micro-goal**: very-early bike before the work day. Clean Y/N: **No.**
- **But the why is the whole story.** I didn't skip it on a whim. I couldn't sleep, got ~2h, and made a deliberate call that riding hard on 2h sleep would be for all the wrong reasons. Honouring that is wisdom, not the whim-failure yesterday's lesson was about. I still got an easy 35 min indoor ride in later in the day, so the day wasn't a write-off.
- **The honest failure sits earlier than the missed bike.** I worked too late and got myself tangled and worked up in the Leakbusters affair. That wound me up, that started the spiral, and that I could have controlled. The 5am willpower was never the lever. The 10pm-onwards choice was.
- **Tomorrow's micro-goal**: protect the evening so the morning is actually possible. No emotionally-charged or high-stakes work late (Leakbusters-type stuff parked by ~21:00), proper wind-down, lights out at a sensible hour. *Clean Y/N tomorrow*: was charged work stopped by 21:00 and did I wind down properly rather than carry work into the night?

## Commitment Continuum, today's position

**Below where I've been — the evening controllable slipped.**

The morning recovery decision was a sound, Compliant-style move. But the day-as-lived dipped because the thing that actually mattered, managing the evening, didn't happen. I let the plan get broken upstream. Honest, not harsh: the recovery call was right; the cause was on me.

## Ownership

**Owned — and owned at the right level.**

To Loren this morning I owned the missed session fully, no excuses. But the ownership that matters is one level up: I caused the late night by taking the Leakbusters affair into the evening and letting it spiral. That is mine. I am owning the *cause I could influence*, not flogging myself over the *outcome* I then couldn't.

**Externalised**: nothing. The night was self-created and I am naming it as such.

## Control the Controllables

**The controllable was the evening, and I didn't control it.**

Once the spiral was running at 1am it was largely uncontrollable — you can't will yourself to sleep. But the influenceable point was hours earlier: don't carry high-stakes, emotionally-charged work into the night. The fear of letting Loren and myself down by missing the session ironically fed the very anxiety that made me miss it. The lever is the wind-down, not the willpower.

The morning decision to abort the ride and sleep was me controlling the controllable well — the one good controllables move of the cycle.

## HF Matrix, today's position

**Overnight: Low Functioning** (negative thinking + negative emotion — the self-created spiral).

**By day, recovered: Resilience** (positive thinking through a flat, tired emotion — sensible recovery call, easy ride, honest reflection). The day pulled itself back out of the quadrant the night dug it into.

## One lesson for tomorrow

**The pressure I put on something can become the very thing that wrecks it. Last night the high-stakes Leakbusters work and the fear of missing the session were the same anxiety, and that anxiety stole the sleep that would have let me make the session. Charged, high-stakes work does not belong in the late evening. Protecting sleep on a bad night is not breaking the commitment, it is keeping the bigger one. The lever is the evening wind-down, never the 5am willpower.**

## Note sent to Loren (morning)

The raw explanation I sent first thing:

> Hi Loren, thought I'd better explain why I wasn't there. What a totally awful night, I got myself so worked up I literally could not get to sleep at all, I was wide awake fighting with myself, it got worse and worse like a spiral and I got myself into a right mess, I just couldn't control it, I was so desperate to get to sleep and to make the session and not let you or myself down but the more I fought to sleep the worse it got. The latest time I remember was about 3am, so think I got to sleep at 3am and then the alarm went off at 5am. I got up and started to get ready but could barely stand up properly, I lay back on the bed and fought with myself some more, in a mess. I then decided getting on the bike after 2 hours sleep would be for all the wrong reasons at this stage and went back to sleep, thankfully I slept like a baby and have just woken up at 9.30am.

## Xhale follow-up to Loren (reflective, post-journal)

```
Follow-up now I have journaled it properly.

The missed session was not really the failure. The real cause was the
night before. I worked too late and got tangled and wound up in a
stressful work matter, and that is what started the spiral and wrecked
the sleep. That part was on me and I could have controlled it.

The thing that struck me: the pressure I was feeling about not letting
you or myself down was the same anxiety that stole the sleep that would
have let me make the session. The fear of missing it is what made me
miss it. The lever was the evening wind down, not willpower at 5am.

Going back to bed instead of riding on 2 hours sleep was the one good
call. Readiness came back to 55 and I still got an easy 35 minutes in
later, so the thread is not broken.

Tomorrow: protect the evening. No charged or high stakes work late,
proper wind down, and bed at a sensible hour so the morning is actually
possible.
```
